        TForBase := Node^.ImmA + 3;
      for Reg := TForBase to TForBase + Node^.ImmC - 1 do begin
        ExtraRef := MakeRef(Reg, 0);
        RenameDef(RS, ExtraRef);
        TrackDef(Reg);
        { Record implicit def so BuildDefNodeMap can populate FDefNodeMap }
        J := Length(SF^.ImplicitDefs);
        SetLength(SF^.ImplicitDefs, J + 1);
        SF^.ImplicitDefs[J].Reg := ExtraRef.Reg;
        SF^.ImplicitDefs[J].Version := ExtraRef.Version;
        SF^.ImplicitDefs[J].ParentNode := Node;
      end;
    end;

    { SELF A B C does: R(A+1) := R(B); R(A) := R(B)[RK(C)]
      Dest already covers R(A), but R(A+1) (the implicit "self" copy) is also
      written.  Without this def, subsequent uses of R(A+1) see a stale version
      from before SELF, inflating its use count and causing spurious emissions. }
    if Node^.Kind = SSA_SELF then begin
      Reg := Node^.ImmA;  { A + 1 was stored in ImmA at lift time }
      if Reg >= 0 then begin
        ExtraRef := MakeRef(Reg, 0);
        RenameDef(RS, ExtraRef);
        TrackDef(Reg);
        { Record implicit def for SELF R(A+1) }
        J := Length(SF^.ImplicitDefs);
        SetLength(SF^.ImplicitDefs, J + 1);
        SF^.ImplicitDefs[J].Reg := ExtraRef.Reg;
        SF^.ImplicitDefs[J].Version := ExtraRef.Version;
        SF^.ImplicitDefs[J].ParentNode := Node;
      end;
    end;

    { CALL with fixed multi-return: C >= 3 means (C-1) return values.
      Dest covers R(A), but R(A+1)..R(A+C-2) are also overwritten by the CALL.
      Without this, a register used as both a call argument and a return slot
      (e.g., R1 holds CLOSURE before CALL, but gets pcall's 2nd return after)
      keeps its pre-CALL SSA version, causing ExprOf to resolve it to the
      argument expression instead of the return value. }
    if (Node^.Kind = SSA_CALL) and (Node^.ImmC >= 3) then begin
      for Reg := Node^.ImmA + 1 to Node^.ImmA + Node^.ImmC - 2 do begin
        ExtraRef := MakeRef(Reg, 0);
        RenameDef(RS, ExtraRef);
        TrackDef(Reg);
        { Record implicit def for CALL extra return register }
        J := Length(SF^.ImplicitDefs);
        SetLength(SF^.ImplicitDefs, J + 1);
        SF^.ImplicitDefs[J].Reg := ExtraRef.Reg;
        SF^.ImplicitDefs[J].Version := ExtraRef.Version;
        SF^.ImplicitDefs[J].ParentNode := Node;
      end;
    end;
  end;

  { Insert deferred LOADNIL pseudo-def nodes into Blk^.Nodes.
    We append them at the end since they share the same PC as the primary
    LOADNIL and their ordering relative to other nodes is not critical -
    they exist solely for LocVar binding (IsMetaOnly = True). }
  if PseudoCount > 0 then begin
    J := Length(Blk^.Nodes);
    SetLength(Blk^.Nodes, J + PseudoCount);
    for I := 0 to PseudoCount - 1 do
      Blk^.Nodes[J + I] := PseudoNodes[I];
  end;

  { Fill Phi-uses in successors }
  for I := 0 to High(Blk^.Succs) do begin
    Succ := Blk^.Succs[I];
    { find BIdx in Succ's predecessor list }
    PredIdx := -1;
    for J := 0 to High(SF^.Blocks[Succ].Preds) do
      if SF^.Blocks[Succ].Preds[J] = BIdx then begin
        PredIdx := J; Break;
      end;
    if PredIdx < 0 then Continue;
    for J := 0 to High(SF^.Blocks[Succ].Nodes) do begin
      Node := SF^.Blocks[Succ].Nodes[J];
      if Node^.Kind <> SSA_PHI then Break;
      Reg := Node^.Dest.Reg;
      if (PredIdx < Length(Node^.Operands)) and (Reg >= 0) then
        Node^.Operands[PredIdx] := MakeRef(Reg, VSPeek(RS.Stack[Reg]));
    end;
  end;

  { Recurse into dominator tree children }
  for I := 0 to High(Blk^.DomChildren) do
    RenameBlock(SF, Blk^.DomChildren[I], RS);

  { Pop definitions pushed in this block }
  for I := NDefs - 1 downto 0 do
    VSPop(RS.Stack[Defs[I]]);
end;

procedure RenamePass(SF: PSSAFunction);
var
  RS : TRenameState;
  I  : Integer;
begin
  SetLength(RS.Counter, SF^.NumRegs);
  SetLength(RS.Stack, SF^.NumRegs);
  { Start all counters at 1, reserving version 0 for "initial value".
    For parameters, version 0 represents the incoming argument.
    For non-parameters, version 0 represents the VM's implicit nil
    (Lua initialises all stack slots to nil).  This ensures that a
    register used before its first definition gets version 0, which
    no SSA node defines, letting ExprOf resolve it to the correct
    initial value (parameter name or 'nil'). }
  for I := 0 to SF^.NumRegs - 1 do begin
    RS.Counter[I] := 1;
    RS.Stack[I].Top := 0;
  end;
  { Push version 0 onto the stack for parameters so that uses of
    a parameter before any reassignment read version 0. }
  for I := 0 to SF^.Proto^.NumParams - 1 do
    VSPush(RS.Stack[I], 0);   { version 0 = the parameter itself }
  if Length(SF^.Blocks) > 0 then
    RenameBlock(SF, 0, RS);
end;

{ ======================================================================
  Version-aware CFG + Lifting (using TSemanticOp / TOpcodeTable)
  ====================================================================== }

procedure ComputeLeadersV(Proto: PProto; const OT: TOpcodeTable;
  var Leaders: array of Boolean);
var
  I, N, Target: Integer;
  Inst: TInstruction;
  SOp: TSemanticOp;
  Bx: Integer;
begin
  N := Length(Proto^.Code);
  Leaders[0] := True;
  I := 0;
  while I < N do begin
    Inst := Proto^.Code[I];
    SOp  := DecodeOp(OT, Inst);
    case SOp of
      semJMP: begin
        if OT.Version >= $54 then
          Target := I + 1 + DecodesJ(OT, Inst)
        else
          Target := I + 1 + DecodesBx(OT, Inst);
        if (Target >= 0) and (Target < N) then Leaders[Target] := True;
        if I + 1 < N then Leaders[I + 1] := True;
      end;
      semEQ, semLT, semLE, semTEST, semTESTSET,
      semEQK, semEQI, semLTI, semLEI, semGTI, semGEI: begin
        if I + 1 < N then Leaders[I + 1] := True;
        if I + 2 < N then Leaders[I + 2] := True;
      end;
      semFORLOOP: begin
        if OT.Version >= $54 then begin
          { 5.4+: ABx format, target = PC + 1 - Bx (backward jump) }
          Bx := DecodeBx(OT, Inst);
          Target := I + 1 - Bx;
        end else
          Target := I + 1 + DecodesBx(OT, Inst);
        if (Target >= 0) and (Target < N) then Leaders[Target] := True;
        if I + 1 < N then Leaders[I + 1] := True;
      end;
      semFORPREP: begin
        if OT.Version >= $54 then begin
          { 5.4+: ABx format, target = PC + 1 + Bx (forward jump) }
          Bx := DecodeBx(OT, Inst);
          Target := I + 1 + Bx;
        end else
          Target := I + 1 + DecodesBx(OT, Inst);
        if (Target >= 0) and (Target < N) then Leaders[Target] := True;
        if I + 1 < N then Leaders[I + 1] := True;
      end;
      semTFORLOOP: begin
        { 5.1 only: followed by JMP }
        if I + 1 < N then Leaders[I + 1] := True;
        if I + 2 < N then Leaders[I + 2] := True;
      end;
      semTFORCALL: begin
        { 5.2+: followed by TFORLOOP54 which has its own jump }
        if I + 1 < N then Leaders[I + 1] := True;
        if I + 2 < N then Leaders[I + 2] := True;
      end;
      semTFORLOOP54: begin
        { 5.2+: ABx/AsBx format, jumps backward }
        if OT.Version >= $54 then begin
          Bx := DecodeBx(OT, Inst);
          Target := I + 1 - Bx;
        end else
          Target := I + 1 + DecodesBx(OT, Inst);
        if (Target >= 0) and (Target < N) then Leaders[Target] := True;
        if I + 1 < N then Leaders[I + 1] := True;
      end;
      semTFORPREP: begin
        { 5.4+: ABx format, target = PC + 1 + Bx }
        Bx := DecodeBx(OT, Inst);
        Target := I + 1 + Bx;
        if (Target >= 0) and (Target < N) then Leaders[Target] := True;
        if I + 1 < N then Leaders[I + 1] := True;
      end;
      semLOADBOOL: begin
        { LOADBOOL with C=1 skips next instruction (5.1-5.3) }
        if DecodeC(OT, Inst) <> 0 then begin
          if I + 1 < N then Leaders[I + 1] := True;
          if I + 2 < N then Leaders[I + 2] := True;
        end;
      end;
      semLFALSESKIP: begin
        { 5.4+: skips next instruction }
        if I + 1 < N then Leaders[I + 1] := True;
        if I + 2 < N then Leaders[I + 2] := True;
      end;
      semRETURN, semRETURN0, semRETURN1, semTAILCALL: begin
        if I + 1 < N then Leaders[I + 1] := True;
      end;
    end;
    Inc(I);
  end;
end;

procedure BuildCFGV(SF: PSSAFunction; const OT: TOpcodeTable);
var
  Proto  : PProto;
  N      : Integer;
  Leaders: array of Boolean;
  PCMap  : array of Integer;
  I, BIdx: Integer;
  Inst   : TInstruction;
  SOp    : TSemanticOp;
  Target : Integer;
  Bx     : Integer;
  NBB    : Integer;
  Blk    : PBasicBlock;

  procedure AddSucc(Src, Dst: Integer);
  var J: Integer;
  begin
    if Dst < 0 then Exit;
    for J := 0 to High(SF^.Blocks[Src].Succs) do
      if SF^.Blocks[Src].Succs[J] = Dst then Exit;
    SetLength(SF^.Blocks[Src].Succs, Length(SF^.Blocks[Src].Succs) + 1);
    SF^.Blocks[Src].Succs[High(SF^.Blocks[Src].Succs)] := Dst;
    SetLength(SF^.Blocks[Dst].Preds, Length(SF^.Blocks[Dst].Preds) + 1);
    SF^.Blocks[Dst].Preds[High(SF^.Blocks[Dst].Preds)] := Src;
  end;

begin
  Proto := SF^.Proto;
  N     := Length(Proto^.Code);
  if N = 0 then Exit;

  SetLength(Leaders, N);
  SetLength(PCMap, N);
  for I := 0 to N - 1 do begin Leaders[I] := False; PCMap[I] := -1; end;

  ComputeLeadersV(Proto, OT, Leaders);

  NBB := 0;
  for I := 0 to N - 1 do
    if Leaders[I] then begin
      PCMap[I] := NBB;
      Inc(NBB);
    end;

  BIdx := -1;
  for I := 0 to N - 1 do begin
    if Leaders[I] then BIdx := PCMap[I];
    PCMap[I] := BIdx;
  end;

  SetLength(SF^.Blocks, NBB);
  for I := 0 to NBB - 1 do begin
    SF^.Blocks[I] := Default(TBasicBlock);
    SF^.Blocks[I].Index   := I;
    SF^.Blocks[I].StartPC := -1;
    SF^.Blocks[I].EndPC   := -1;
    SF^.Blocks[I].IDom    := -1;
  end;

  BIdx := -1;
  for I := 0 to N - 1 do begin
    if Leaders[I] then begin
      BIdx := PCMap[I];
      SF^.Blocks[BIdx].StartPC := I;
    end;
    SF^.Blocks[BIdx].EndPC := I;
  end;

  { Add edges }
  for BIdx := 0 to NBB - 1 do begin
    Blk  := @SF^.Blocks[BIdx];
    I    := Blk^.EndPC;
    Inst := Proto^.Code[I];
    SOp  := DecodeOp(OT, Inst);
    case SOp of
      semJMP: begin
        if OT.Version >= $54 then
          Target := I + 1 + DecodesJ(OT, Inst)
        else
          Target := I + 1 + DecodesBx(OT, Inst);
        AddSucc(BIdx, PCToBlock(PCMap, Target));
      end;
      semEQ, semLT, semLE, semTEST, semTESTSET,
      semEQK, semEQI, semLTI, semLEI, semGTI, semGEI: begin
        AddSucc(BIdx, PCToBlock(PCMap, I + 1));
        AddSucc(BIdx, PCToBlock(PCMap, I + 2));
      end;
      semFORLOOP: begin
        if OT.Version >= $54 then begin
          Bx := DecodeBx(OT, Inst);
          Target := I + 1 - Bx;
        end else
          Target := I + 1 + DecodesBx(OT, Inst);
        AddSucc(BIdx, PCToBlock(PCMap, Target));
        AddSucc(BIdx, PCToBlock(PCMap, I + 1));
      end;
      semFORPREP: begin
        if OT.Version >= $54 then begin
          Bx := DecodeBx(OT, Inst);
          Target := I + 1 + Bx;
        end else
          Target := I + 1 + DecodesBx(OT, Inst);
        AddSucc(BIdx, PCToBlock(PCMap, Target));
      end;
      semTFORLOOP: begin
        { 5.1: followed by JMP }
        AddSucc(BIdx, PCToBlock(PCMap, I + 1));
        AddSucc(BIdx, PCToBlock(PCMap, I + 2));
      end;
      semTFORCALL: begin
        { 5.2+: followed by TFORLOOP54 }
        AddSucc(BIdx, PCToBlock(PCMap, I + 1));
        AddSucc(BIdx, PCToBlock(PCMap, I + 2));
      end;
      semTFORLOOP54: begin
        if OT.Version >= $54 then begin
          Bx := DecodeBx(OT, Inst);
          Target := I + 1 - Bx;
        end else
          Target := I + 1 + DecodesBx(OT, Inst);
        AddSucc(BIdx, PCToBlock(PCMap, Target));
        AddSucc(BIdx, PCToBlock(PCMap, I + 1));
      end;
      semTFORPREP: begin
        Bx := DecodeBx(OT, Inst);
        Target := I + 1 + Bx;
        AddSucc(BIdx, PCToBlock(PCMap, Target));
      end;
      semLOADBOOL: begin
        if DecodeC(OT, Inst) <> 0 then
          AddSucc(BIdx, PCToBlock(PCMap, I + 2))
        else if I + 1 < N then
          AddSucc(BIdx, PCToBlock(PCMap, I + 1));
      end;
      semLFALSESKIP: begin
        AddSucc(BIdx, PCToBlock(PCMap, I + 2));
      end;
      semRETURN, semRETURN0, semRETURN1, semTAILCALL:
        { no successors } ;
    else
      if I + 1 < N then
        AddSucc(BIdx, PCToBlock(PCMap, I + 1));
    end;
  end;
end;

{ ======================================================================
  Version-aware instruction lifting
  ====================================================================== }

function IsEnvUpval(Proto: PProto; Idx: Integer): Boolean;
{ Check if upvalue at Idx is _ENV. In 5.2+ nested closures, _ENV can be at
  any upvalue index, not just 0. Falls back to index=0 if no debug names. }
begin
  if (Idx >= 0) and (Idx < Length(Proto^.UpvalueNames)) then
    Result := Proto^.UpvalueNames[Idx] = '_ENV'
  else
    Result := (Idx = 0);  { no debug info: assume index 0 is _ENV (common case) }
end;

procedure LiftBlockV(SF: PSSAFunction; BIdx: Integer; const OT: TOpcodeTable);
var
  Proto : PProto;
  Blk   : PBasicBlock;
  PC    : Integer;
  Inst  : TInstruction;
  SOp   : TSemanticOp;
  A, B, C, Bx, sBx, sJ: Integer;
  sB, sC: Integer;
  KFlag : Boolean;
  Node  : PSSANode;
  Line  : Integer;
  J, ArgCount, TopSetReg: Integer;
  NextInst: TInstruction;
  AxVal : Integer;

  procedure Add(N: PSSANode);
  begin
    SetLength(Blk^.Nodes, Length(Blk^.Nodes) + 1);
    Blk^.Nodes[High(Blk^.Nodes)] := N;
    SetLength(SF^.AllNodes, Length(SF^.AllNodes) + 1);
    SF^.AllNodes[High(SF^.AllNodes)] := N;
  end;

begin
  Proto := SF^.Proto;
  Blk   := @SF^.Blocks[BIdx];
  PC := Blk^.StartPC;
  while PC <= Blk^.EndPC do begin
    Inst := Proto^.Code[PC];
    SOp  := DecodeOp(OT, Inst);
    A    := DecodeA(OT, Inst);
    B    := DecodeB(OT, Inst);
    C    := DecodeC(OT, Inst);
    Bx   := DecodeBx(OT, Inst);
    sBx  := DecodesBx(OT, Inst);
    KFlag := DecodeK(OT, Inst);
    { For ivABC format (5.5 NEWTABLE/SETLIST), re-decode B and C with
      correct bit widths: vB=6 bits, vC=10 bits instead of standard 8/8 }
    if IsIvABCFormat(OT, Inst) then begin
      B := DecodeVB(OT, Inst);
      C := DecodeVC(OT, Inst);
    end;
    if PC < Length(Proto^.LineInfo) then Line := Proto^.LineInfo[PC]
    else Line := -1;

    case SOp of
      { ---- MOVE ---- }
      semMOVE: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_MOVE, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.OpA  := MakeRef(B, 0);
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- LOADK ---- }
      semLOADK: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_LOADK, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest     := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.ConstIdx := Bx;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- LOADKX ---- }
      semLOADKX: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_LOADK, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A, 0);
        { Peek next instruction for EXTRAARG Ax value }
        if PC + 1 < Length(Proto^.Code) then begin
          NextInst := Proto^.Code[PC + 1];
          AxVal := DecodeAx(OT, NextInst);
          Node^.ConstIdx := AxVal;
        end else
          Node^.ConstIdx := Bx;
        Add(Node);
        { Emit NOP for the EXTRAARG instruction }
        Inc(PC);
        if PC <= Blk^.EndPC then begin
          Node := NewNode(SSA_NOP, PC, -1);
          Add(Node);
        end;
      end;

      { ---- LOADBOOL ---- }
      semLOADBOOL: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_LOADBOOL, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.ImmA := B;  { 0 or 1 }
        Node^.ImmB := C;  { skip flag }
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- LOADNIL ---- }
      semLOADNIL: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_LOADNIL, PC, Line);
        if OT.Version >= $52 then begin
          { 5.2+: LOADNIL A B means R(A)..R(A+B) := nil }
          Node^.ImmA := A;
          Node^.ImmB := A + B;
        end else begin
          { 5.1: LOADNIL A B means R(A)..R(B) := nil }
          Node^.ImmA := A;
          Node^.ImmB := B;
        end;
        Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- LOADI (5.4+) ---- }
      semLOADI: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_LOADK, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest     := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.ConstIdx := AddSyntheticConst(Proto, sBx);
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- LOADF (5.4+) ---- }
      semLOADF: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_LOADK, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest     := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.ConstIdx := AddSyntheticFloatConst(Proto, Double(sBx));
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- LOADFALSE (5.4+) ---- }
      semLOADFALSE: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_LOADBOOL, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.ImmA := 0;  { false }
        Node^.ImmB := 0;  { no skip }
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- LOADTRUE (5.4+) ---- }
      semLOADTRUE: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_LOADBOOL, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.ImmA := 1;  { true }
        Node^.ImmB := 0;  { no skip }
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- LFALSESKIP (5.4+) ---- }
      semLFALSESKIP: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_LOADBOOL, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.ImmA := 0;  { false }
        Node^.ImmB := 1;  { skip next instruction }
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- GETUPVAL ---- }
      semGETUPVAL: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_GETUPVAL, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest     := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.UpvalIdx := B;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- SETUPVAL ---- }
      semSETUPVAL: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_SETUPVAL, PC, Line);
        Node^.OpA      := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.UpvalIdx := B;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- GETGLOBAL (5.1 only) ---- }
      semGETGLOBAL: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_GETGLOBAL, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest     := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.ConstIdx := Bx;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- SETGLOBAL (5.1 only) ---- }
      semSETGLOBAL: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_SETGLOBAL, PC, Line);
        Node^.OpA      := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.ConstIdx := Bx;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- GETTABUP (5.2+) ---- }
      semGETTABUP: begin
        if IsEnvUpval(Proto, B) then begin
          { Upval is _ENV: normalize to GETGLOBAL }
          Node := NewNode(SSA_GETGLOBAL, PC, Line);
          Node^.Dest     := MakeRef(A, 0);
          if OT.HasKFlag then
            Node^.ConstIdx := C
          else
            Node^.ConstIdx := RKIndex(OT, C);
        end else begin
          { Generic upvalue table access }
          Node := NewNode(SSA_GETTABLE, PC, Line);
          Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A, 0);
          { OpA references the upvalue; mark as upval ref using UpvalIdx }
          Node^.UpvalIdx := B;
          Node^.OpA  := SSA_NO_REF; { no register for upval }
          if OT.HasKFlag then
            Node^.OpB := MakeConstRef(C)
          else
            Node^.OpB := RKRefV(OT, C);
        end;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- SETTABUP (5.2+) ---- }
      semSETTABUP: begin
        if IsEnvUpval(Proto, A) then begin
          { Upval is _ENV: normalize to SETGLOBAL }
          Node := NewNode(SSA_SETGLOBAL, PC, Line);
          if OT.HasKFlag then begin
            Node^.ConstIdx := B;
            if KFlag then
              Node^.OpA := MakeConstRef(C)
            else
              Node^.OpA := MakeRef(C, 0);
          end else begin
            Node^.ConstIdx := RKIndex(OT, B);
            Node^.OpA := RKRefV(OT, C);
          end;
        end else begin
          { Generic upvalue table set }
          Node := NewNode(SSA_SETTABLE, PC, Line);
          Node^.UpvalIdx := A;
          Node^.OpA := SSA_NO_REF;
          if OT.HasKFlag then begin
            Node^.OpB := MakeConstRef(B);
            if KFlag then
              Node^.OpC := MakeConstRef(C)
            else
              Node^.OpC := MakeRef(C, 0);
          end else begin
            Node^.OpB := RKRefV(OT, B);
            Node^.OpC := RKRefV(OT, C);
          end;
        end;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- GETTABLE ---- }
      semGETTABLE: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_GETTABLE, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.OpA  := MakeRef(B, 0);
        if OT.HasKFlag then
          Node^.OpB := MakeRef(C, 0)  { 5.4+: C is always register }
        else
          Node^.OpB := RKRefV(OT, C);
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- GETI (5.4+) ---- }
      semGETI: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_GETTABLE, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.OpA  := MakeRef(B, 0);
        Node^.OpB  := MakeConstRef(AddSyntheticConst(Proto, C));
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- GETFIELD (5.4+) ---- }
      semGETFIELD: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_GETTABLE, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.OpA  := MakeRef(B, 0);
        Node^.OpB  := MakeConstRef(C);
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- SETTABLE ---- }
      semSETTABLE: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_SETTABLE, PC, Line);
        Node^.OpA := MakeRef(A, 0);
        if OT.HasKFlag then begin
          Node^.OpB := MakeRef(B, 0);
          if KFlag then
            Node^.OpC := MakeConstRef(C)
          else
            Node^.OpC := MakeRef(C, 0);
        end else begin
          Node^.OpB := RKRefV(OT, B);
          Node^.OpC := RKRefV(OT, C);
        end;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- SETI (5.4+) ---- }
      semSETI: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_SETTABLE, PC, Line);
        Node^.OpA := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.OpB := MakeConstRef(AddSyntheticConst(Proto, B));
        if KFlag then
          Node^.OpC := MakeConstRef(C)
        else
          Node^.OpC := MakeRef(C, 0);
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- SETFIELD (5.4+) ---- }
      semSETFIELD: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_SETTABLE, PC, Line);
        Node^.OpA := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.OpB := MakeConstRef(B);
        if KFlag then
          Node^.OpC := MakeConstRef(C)
        else
          Node^.OpC := MakeRef(C, 0);
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- NEWTABLE ---- }
      semNEWTABLE: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_NEWTABLE, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.ImmA := B;
        Node^.ImmB := C;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- SELF ---- }
      semSELF: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_SELF, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.OpA  := MakeRef(B, 0);
        if OT.Version >= $55 then
          { Lua 5.5: SELF A B C - C is always K[C]:shortstring (like GETFIELD) }
          Node^.OpB := MakeConstRef(C)
        else if OT.HasKFlag then begin
          { Lua 5.4: SELF A B C - RK(C) with k-flag }
          if KFlag then
            Node^.OpB := MakeConstRef(C)
          else
            Node^.OpB := MakeRef(C, 0);
        end else
          Node^.OpB := RKRefV(OT, C);
        Node^.ImmA := A + 1;  { self slot }
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- Binary arithmetic (register operands) ---- }
      semADD, semSUB, semMUL, semDIV, semMOD, semPOW,
      semIDIV, semBAND, semBOR, semBXOR, semSHL, semSHR: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_BINOP, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A, 0);
        if OT.HasKFlag then begin
          Node^.OpA := MakeRef(B, 0);
          Node^.OpB := MakeRef(C, 0);
        end else begin
          Node^.OpA := RKRefV(OT, B);
          Node^.OpB := RKRefV(OT, C);
        end;
        case SOp of
          semADD:  Node^.BinOp := bopAdd;
          semSUB:  Node^.BinOp := bopSub;
          semMUL:  Node^.BinOp := bopMul;
          semDIV:  Node^.BinOp := bopDiv;
          semMOD:  Node^.BinOp := bopMod;
          semPOW:  Node^.BinOp := bopPow;
          semIDIV: Node^.BinOp := bopIDiv;
          semBAND: Node^.BinOp := bopBAnd;
          semBOR:  Node^.BinOp := bopBOr;
          semBXOR: Node^.BinOp := bopBXor;
          semSHL:  Node^.BinOp := bopSHL;
          semSHR:  Node^.BinOp := bopSHR;
        end;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- Constant-keyed arithmetic (5.4+) ---- }
      semADDK, semSUBK, semMULK, semMODK, semPOWK,
      semDIVK, semIDIVK, semBANDK, semBORK, semBXORK: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_BINOP, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.OpA  := MakeRef(B, 0);
        Node^.OpB  := MakeConstRef(C);
        case SOp of
          semADDK:  Node^.BinOp := bopAdd;
          semSUBK:  Node^.BinOp := bopSub;
          semMULK:  Node^.BinOp := bopMul;
          semMODK:  Node^.BinOp := bopMod;
          semPOWK:  Node^.BinOp := bopPow;
          semDIVK:  Node^.BinOp := bopDiv;
          semIDIVK: Node^.BinOp := bopIDiv;
          semBANDK: Node^.BinOp := bopBAnd;
          semBORK:  Node^.BinOp := bopBOr;
          semBXORK: Node^.BinOp := bopBXor;
        end;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- ADDI (5.4+) ---- }
      semADDI: begin
        sC := DecodeSC(OT, Inst);
        Node := NewNode(SSA_BINOP, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest  := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.OpA   := MakeRef(B, 0);
        Node^.OpB   := MakeConstRef(AddSyntheticConst(Proto, sC));
        Node^.BinOp := bopAdd;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- SHRI (5.4+) ---- }
      semSHRI: begin
        sC := DecodeSC(OT, Inst);
        Node := NewNode(SSA_BINOP, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest  := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.OpA   := MakeRef(B, 0);
        Node^.OpB   := MakeConstRef(AddSyntheticConst(Proto, sC));
        Node^.BinOp := bopSHR;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- SHLI (5.4+): sC << R(B), operands reversed ---- }
      semSHLI: begin
        sC := DecodeSC(OT, Inst);
        Node := NewNode(SSA_BINOP, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest  := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.OpA   := MakeConstRef(AddSyntheticConst(Proto, sC));
        Node^.OpB   := MakeRef(B, 0);
        Node^.BinOp := bopSHL;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- Unary ops ---- }
      semUNM, semNOT, semLEN, semBNOT: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_UNOP, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.OpA  := MakeRef(B, 0);
        case SOp of
          semUNM:  Node^.UnOp := uopNeg;
          semNOT:  Node^.UnOp := uopNot;
          semLEN:  Node^.UnOp := uopLen;
          semBNOT: Node^.UnOp := uopBNot;
        end;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- CONCAT ---- }
      semCONCAT: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_CONCAT, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A, 0);
        if OT.Version >= $54 then begin
          { 5.4+: CONCAT A B -> R[A] := R[A].. ... ..R[A+B-1] (B operands) }
          Node^.ImmA := A;          { first register }
          Node^.ImmB := A + B - 1;  { last register }
        end else begin
          { 5.1-5.3: CONCAT A B C -> R(A) := R(B)..R(C) }
          Node^.ImmA := B;
          Node^.ImmB := C;
        end;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- JMP ---- }
      semJMP: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_JUMP, PC, Line);
        if OT.Version >= $54 then begin
          sJ := DecodesJ(OT, Inst);
          Node^.TargetTrue := PC + 1 + sJ;
        end else
          Node^.TargetTrue := PC + 1 + sBx;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- EQ, LT, LE ---- }
      semEQ, semLT, semLE: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_BRANCH, PC, Line);
        case SOp of
          semEQ: Node^.RelOp := ropEQ;
          semLT: Node^.RelOp := ropLT;
          semLE: Node^.RelOp := ropLE;
        end;
        if OT.HasKFlag then begin
          { 5.4+: operands are registers A and B, invert via k-flag }
          Node^.OpA    := MakeRef(A, 0);
          Node^.OpB    := MakeRef(B, 0);
          Node^.Invert := KFlag;
        end else begin
          { 5.1-5.3: operands are RK(B) and RK(C), invert = A!=0 }
          Node^.OpA    := RKRefV(OT, B);
          Node^.OpB    := RKRefV(OT, C);
          Node^.Invert := A <> 0;
        end;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- EQK (5.4+) ---- }
      semEQK: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_BRANCH, PC, Line);
        Node^.RelOp  := ropEQ;
        Node^.OpA    := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.OpB    := MakeConstRef(B);
        Node^.Invert := KFlag;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- EQI (5.4+) ---- }
      semEQI: begin
        sB := DecodeSB(OT, Inst);
        Node := NewNode(SSA_BRANCH, PC, Line);
        Node^.RelOp  := ropEQ;
        Node^.OpA    := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.OpB    := MakeConstRef(AddSyntheticConst(Proto, sB));
        Node^.Invert := KFlag;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- LTI (5.4+) ---- }
      semLTI: begin
        sB := DecodeSB(OT, Inst);
        Node := NewNode(SSA_BRANCH, PC, Line);
        Node^.RelOp  := ropLT;
        Node^.OpA    := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.OpB    := MakeConstRef(AddSyntheticConst(Proto, sB));
        Node^.Invert := KFlag;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- LEI (5.4+) ---- }
      semLEI: begin
        sB := DecodeSB(OT, Inst);
        Node := NewNode(SSA_BRANCH, PC, Line);
        Node^.RelOp  := ropLE;
        Node^.OpA    := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.OpB    := MakeConstRef(AddSyntheticConst(Proto, sB));
        Node^.Invert := KFlag;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- GTI (5.4+): R(A) > sB => sB < R(A) ---- }
      semGTI: begin
        sB := DecodeSB(OT, Inst);
        Node := NewNode(SSA_BRANCH, PC, Line);
        Node^.RelOp  := ropLT;
        { Swap operands: GT(A, sB) becomes LT(sB, A) }
        Node^.OpA    := MakeConstRef(AddSyntheticConst(Proto, sB));
        Node^.OpB    := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.Invert := KFlag;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- GEI (5.4+): R(A) >= sB => sB <= R(A) ---- }
      semGEI: begin
        sB := DecodeSB(OT, Inst);
        Node := NewNode(SSA_BRANCH, PC, Line);
        Node^.RelOp  := ropLE;
        { Swap operands: GE(A, sB) becomes LE(sB, A) }
        Node^.OpA    := MakeConstRef(AddSyntheticConst(Proto, sB));
        Node^.OpB    := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.Invert := KFlag;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- TEST ---- }
      semTEST: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_BRANCH, PC, Line);
        Node^.RelOp := ropTest;
        Node^.OpA   := MakeRef(A, 0);
        if OT.HasKFlag then
          Node^.Invert := KFlag
        else
          Node^.Invert := C <> 0;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- TESTSET ---- }
      semTESTSET: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_BRANCH, PC, Line);
        Node^.RelOp := ropTestSet;
        Node^.Dest  := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.OpA   := MakeRef(B, 0);
        if OT.HasKFlag then
          Node^.Invert := KFlag
        else
          Node^.Invert := C <> 0;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- CALL ---- }
      semCALL: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_CALL, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.OpA  := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.ImmA := A;
        Node^.ImmB := B;
        Node^.ImmC := C;
        if B > 1 then begin
          SetLength(Node^.ArgRefs, B - 1);
          for J := 0 to B - 2 do
            Node^.ArgRefs[J] := MakeRef(A + 1 + J, 0);
        end else if B = 0 then begin
          TopSetReg := -1;
          for J := High(Blk^.Nodes) downto 0 do begin
            if (Blk^.Nodes[J]^.Kind = SSA_CALL) and (Blk^.Nodes[J]^.ImmC = 0) then begin
              TopSetReg := Blk^.Nodes[J]^.ImmA;
              Break;
            end;
            if (Blk^.Nodes[J]^.Kind = SSA_VARARG) and
               (Blk^.Nodes[J]^.ImmB = 0) then begin
              TopSetReg := Blk^.Nodes[J]^.Dest.Reg;
              Break;
            end;
          end;
          if TopSetReg > A then begin
            ArgCount := TopSetReg - A;
            SetLength(Node^.ArgRefs, ArgCount);
            for J := 0 to ArgCount - 1 do
              Node^.ArgRefs[J] := MakeRef(A + 1 + J, 0);
          end;
        end;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- TAILCALL ---- }
      semTAILCALL: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_TAILCALL, PC, Line);
        Node^.OpA  := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.ImmA := A;
        Node^.ImmB := B;
        Node^.ImmC := C;
        if B > 1 then begin
          SetLength(Node^.ArgRefs, B - 1);
          for J := 0 to B - 2 do
            Node^.ArgRefs[J] := MakeRef(A + 1 + J, 0);
        end else if B = 0 then begin
          { Variable args: R(A+1) to stack top.
            Scan backward for the stack-top-setting instruction. }
          TopSetReg := -1;
          for J := High(Blk^.Nodes) downto 0 do begin
            if (Blk^.Nodes[J]^.Kind = SSA_CALL) and (Blk^.Nodes[J]^.ImmC = 0) then begin
              TopSetReg := Blk^.Nodes[J]^.ImmA;
              Break;
            end;
            if (Blk^.Nodes[J]^.Kind = SSA_VARARG) and
               (Blk^.Nodes[J]^.ImmB = 0) then begin
              TopSetReg := Blk^.Nodes[J]^.Dest.Reg;
              Break;
            end;
          end;
          if TopSetReg > A then begin
            ArgCount := TopSetReg - A;
            SetLength(Node^.ArgRefs, ArgCount);
            for J := 0 to ArgCount - 1 do
              Node^.ArgRefs[J] := MakeRef(A + 1 + J, 0);
          end;
        end;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- RETURN ---- }
      semRETURN: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_RETURN, PC, Line);
        Node^.ImmA := A;
        Node^.ImmB := B;
        if B > 1 then begin
          SetLength(Node^.ArgRefs, B - 1);
          for J := 0 to B - 2 do
            Node^.ArgRefs[J] := MakeRef(A + J, 0);
        end else if B = 0 then begin
          TopSetReg := -1;
          for J := High(Blk^.Nodes) downto 0 do begin
            if (Blk^.Nodes[J]^.Kind = SSA_CALL) and (Blk^.Nodes[J]^.ImmC = 0) then begin
              TopSetReg := Blk^.Nodes[J]^.ImmA;
              Break;
            end;
            if (Blk^.Nodes[J]^.Kind = SSA_VARARG) and
               (Blk^.Nodes[J]^.ImmB = 0) then begin
              TopSetReg := Blk^.Nodes[J]^.Dest.Reg;
              Break;
            end;
          end;
          if TopSetReg >= A then begin
            ArgCount := TopSetReg - A + 1;
            SetLength(Node^.ArgRefs, ArgCount);
            for J := 0 to ArgCount - 1 do
              Node^.ArgRefs[J] := MakeRef(A + J, 0);
          end;
        end;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- RETURN0 (5.4+) ---- }
      semRETURN0: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_RETURN, PC, Line);
        Node^.ImmA := 0;
        Node^.ImmB := 1;  { B=1 means no return values }
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- RETURN1 (5.4+) ---- }
      semRETURN1: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_RETURN, PC, Line);
        Node^.ImmA := A;
        Node^.ImmB := 2;  { B=2 means one return value }
        SetLength(Node^.ArgRefs, 1);
        Node^.ArgRefs[0] := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- FORLOOP ---- }
      semFORLOOP: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_FORLOOP, PC, Line);
        Node^.ImmA := A;
        { Lua 5.1-5.4: 3 internal vars, user var at R(A+3)
          Lua 5.5:      2 internal vars, user var at R(A+2) }
        if OT.Version >= $55 then
          Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A + 2, 0)
        else
          Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A + 3, 0);
        if OT.Version >= $54 then begin
          Bx := DecodeBx(OT, Inst);
          Node^.TargetTrue := PC + 1 - Bx;
        end else
          Node^.TargetTrue := PC + 1 + sBx;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- FORPREP ---- }
      semFORPREP: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_FORPREP, PC, Line);
        Node^.ImmA := A;
        Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A, 0);
        if OT.Version >= $54 then begin
          Bx := DecodeBx(OT, Inst);
          Node^.TargetTrue := PC + 1 + Bx;
        end else
          Node^.TargetTrue := PC + 1 + sBx;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- TFORLOOP (5.1 only) ---- }
      semTFORLOOP: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_TFORLOOP, PC, Line);
        Node^.ImmA := A;
        Node^.ImmC := C;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- TFORCALL (5.2+) ---- }
      semTFORCALL: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_TFORLOOP, PC, Line);
        Node^.ImmA := A;
        Node^.ImmC := C;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- TFORLOOP54 (5.2+) ---- }
      semTFORLOOP54: begin
        { Loop-back instruction for generic for; emit as FORLOOP-like }
        Node := NewNode(SSA_FORLOOP, PC, Line);
        Node^.ImmA := A;
        Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A, 0);
        if OT.Version >= $54 then begin
          Bx := DecodeBx(OT, Inst);
          Node^.TargetTrue := PC + 1 - Bx;
        end else
          Node^.TargetTrue := PC + 1 + DecodesBx(OT, Inst);
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- TFORPREP (5.4+) ---- }
      semTFORPREP: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_JUMP, PC, Line);
        Bx := DecodeBx(OT, Inst);
        Node^.TargetTrue := PC + 1 + Bx;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- SETLIST ---- }
      semSETLIST: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_SETLIST, PC, Line);
        Node^.ImmA := A;
        Node^.ImmB := B;
        Node^.ImmC := C;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- CLOSE ---- }
      semCLOSE: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_CLOSE, PC, Line);
        Node^.ImmA := A;
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- TBC (5.4+) ---- }
      semTBC: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_NOP, PC, Line);
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- CLOSURE ---- }
      semCLOSURE: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_CLOSURE, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest     := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.ConstIdx := Bx;
        Add(Node);
        { 5.1 only: CLOSURE is followed by pseudo-instructions for upvalue capture }
        if OT.Version < $52 then begin
          if Bx < Length(Proto^.P) then begin
            for J := 1 to Proto^.P[Bx]^.NUps do begin
              Inc(PC);
              if PC > Blk^.EndPC then Break;
              Node := NewNode(SSA_NOP, PC, -1);
              Add(Node);
            end;
          end;
        end;
      end;

      { ---- VARARG ---- }
      semVARARG: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_VARARG, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.ImmA := A;
        { In 5.4+, VARARG A C uses the C field for the count;
          in 5.1-5.3, VARARG A B uses the B field. }
        if OT.Version >= $54 then
          Node^.ImmB := C
        else
          Node^.ImmB := B;
        if OT.Version >= $55 then
          Node^.ImmC := B; { hidden vararg table register when k is set }
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- GETVARG (5.5) ---- }
      semGETVARG: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_GETTABLE, PC, Line);
        Node^.Dest := MakeRef(A, 0);
        Node^.OpA  := MakeRef(B, 0);
        Node^.OpB  := MakeRef(C, 0);
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- VARARGPREP (5.4+) ---- }
      semVARARGPREP: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_NOP, PC, Line);
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- Metamethod hints (5.4+) ---- }
      semMMBIN, semMMBINI, semMMBINK: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_NOP, PC, Line);
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- EXTRAARG ---- }
      semEXTRAARG: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_NOP, PC, Line);
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- ERRNNIL (5.5) ---- }
      semERRNNIL: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_NOP, PC, Line);
        Add(Node);
      end;

      { ---- Unknown ---- }
      semUNKNOWN: begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_NOP, PC, Line);
        Add(Node);
      end;
    else
      begin
        Node := NewNode(SSA_NOP, PC, Line);
        Add(Node);
      end;
    end; { case SOp }
    Inc(PC);
  end; { while PC }
end;

procedure LiftAllBlocksV(SF: PSSAFunction; const OT: TOpcodeTable);
var
  I: Integer;
begin
  for I := 0 to High(SF^.Blocks) do
    LiftBlockV(SF, I, OT);
end;

{ ======================================================================
  BuildSSAFunction  (public entry points)
  ====================================================================== }

function BuildSSAFunction(Proto: PProto): PSSAFunction;
begin
  New(Result);
  Result^ := Default(TSSAFunction);
  Result^.Proto   := Proto;
  Result^.NumRegs := Proto^.MaxStack;
  Result^.LuaVersion := $51;  { legacy path = Lua 5.1 }

  BuildCFG(Result);
  ComputeDominators(Result);
  ComputeDominanceFrontier(Result);
  LiftAllBlocks(Result);
  InsertPhiFunctions(Result);
  RenamePass(Result);
end;

function BuildSSAFunction(Proto: PProto; const OT: POpcodeTable): PSSAFunction;
begin
  if OT = nil then begin
    Result := BuildSSAFunction(Proto);
    Exit;
  end;

  New(Result);
  Result^ := Default(TSSAFunction);
  Result^.Proto   := Proto;
  Result^.NumRegs := Proto^.MaxStack;
  Result^.LuaVersion := OT^.Version;

  BuildCFGV(Result, OT^);
  ComputeDominators(Result);
  ComputeDominanceFrontier(Result);
  LiftAllBlocksV(Result, OT^);
  InsertPhiFunctions(Result);
  RenamePass(Result);
end;

{ ======================================================================
  DumpSSA
  ====================================================================== }

procedure DumpSSA(const F: PSSAFunction);
var
  I, J, K : Integer;
  Blk  : PBasicBlock;
  Node : PSSANode;

  function KindName(K: TSSAKind): AnsiString;
  begin
    case K of
      SSA_NOP:      Result := 'NOP';
      SSA_PHI:      Result := 'PHI';
      SSA_MOVE:     Result := 'MOVE';
      SSA_LOADK:    Result := 'LOADK';
      SSA_LOADBOOL: Result := 'LOADBOOL';
      SSA_LOADNIL:  Result := 'LOADNIL';
      SSA_GETUPVAL: Result := 'GETUPVAL';
      SSA_GETGLOBAL:Result := 'GETGLOBAL';
      SSA_GETTABLE: Result := 'GETTABLE';
      SSA_SETGLOBAL:Result := 'SETGLOBAL';
      SSA_SETUPVAL: Result := 'SETUPVAL';
      SSA_SETTABLE: Result := 'SETTABLE';
      SSA_NEWTABLE: Result := 'NEWTABLE';
      SSA_SELF:     Result := 'SELF';
      SSA_BINOP:    Result := 'BINOP';
      SSA_UNOP:     Result := 'UNOP';
      SSA_CONCAT:   Result := 'CONCAT';
      SSA_JUMP:     Result := 'JUMP';
      SSA_BRANCH:   Result := 'BRANCH';
      SSA_CALL:     Result := 'CALL';
      SSA_TAILCALL: Result := 'TAILCALL';
      SSA_RETURN:   Result := 'RETURN';
      SSA_FORPREP:  Result := 'FORPREP';
      SSA_FORLOOP:  Result := 'FORLOOP';
      SSA_TFORLOOP: Result := 'TFORLOOP';
      SSA_SETLIST:  Result := 'SETLIST';
      SSA_CLOSE:    Result := 'CLOSE';
      SSA_CLOSURE:  Result := 'CLOSURE';
      SSA_VARARG:   Result := 'VARARG';
    end;
  end;

begin
  WriteLn('-- SSA Dump --');
  for I := 0 to High(F^.Blocks) do begin
    Blk := @F^.Blocks[I];
    Write('BB', I, ' [pc ', Blk^.StartPC, '..', Blk^.EndPC, ']');
    Write(' preds:[');
    for J := 0 to High(Blk^.Preds) do begin
      if J > 0 then Write(',');
      Write(Blk^.Preds[J]);
    end;
    Write('] succs:[');
    for J := 0 to High(Blk^.Succs) do begin
      if J > 0 then Write(',');
      Write(Blk^.Succs[J]);
    end;
    Write('] idom=', Blk^.IDom);
    WriteLn;
    for J := 0 to High(Blk^.Nodes) do begin
      Node := Blk^.Nodes[J];
      Write('  [', Node^.PC, '] ', KindName(Node^.Kind), ' ');
      if Node^.Dest.Reg >= 0 then
        Write(RefToStr(Node^.Dest), ' := ');
      if Node^.Kind = SSA_PHI then begin
        Write('phi(');
        for K := 0 to High(Node^.Operands) do begin
          if K > 0 then Write(', ');
          Write(RefToStr(Node^.Operands[K]), ' from BB', Node^.PhiBlocks[K]);
        end;
        Write(')');
      end else begin
        { Show operator detail for BINOP and UNOP }
        if Node^.Kind = SSA_BINOP then begin
          case Node^.BinOp of
            bopAdd:  Write('[+] ');
            bopSub:  Write('[-] ');
            bopMul:  Write('[*] ');
            bopDiv:  Write('[/] ');
            bopMod:  Write('[%] ');
            bopPow:  Write('[^] ');
            bopIDiv: Write('[//] ');
            bopBAnd: Write('[&] ');
            bopBOr:  Write('[|] ');
            bopBXor: Write('[~] ');
            bopSHL:  Write('[<<] ');
            bopSHR:  Write('[>>] ');
          end;
        end;
        if Node^.Kind = SSA_UNOP then begin
          case Node^.UnOp of
            uopNeg:  Write('[-] ');
            uopNot:  Write('[not] ');
            uopLen:  Write('[#] ');
            uopBNot: Write('[~] ');
          end;
        end;
        if Node^.Kind = SSA_BRANCH then begin
          case Node^.RelOp of
            ropEQ:      Write('[==] ');
            ropLT:      Write('[<] ');
            ropLE:      Write('[<=] ');
            ropTest:    Write('[test] ');
            ropTestSet: Write('[testset] ');
          end;
          if Node^.Invert then Write('inv ');
        end;
        if Node^.OpA.Reg >= -1 then Write(RefToStr(Node^.OpA), ' ');
        if Node^.OpB.Reg >= -1 then Write(RefToStr(Node^.OpB), ' ');
        if Node^.OpC.Reg >= -1 then Write(RefToStr(Node^.OpC), ' ');
      end;
      WriteLn;
    end;
  end;
  WriteLn;
end;

{ ======================================================================
  FreeSSAFunction
  ====================================================================== }

procedure FreeSSAFunction(F: PSSAFunction);
var
  I: Integer;
begin
  if F = nil then Exit;
  for I := 0 to High(F^.AllNodes) do begin
    SetLength(F^.AllNodes[I]^.Operands, 0);
    SetLength(F^.AllNodes[I]^.PhiBlocks, 0);
    Dispose(F^.AllNodes[I]);
  end;
  SetLength(F^.AllNodes, 0);
  for I := 0 to High(F^.Blocks) do begin
    SetLength(F^.Blocks[I].Nodes, 0);
    SetLength(F^.Blocks[I].Succs, 0);
    SetLength(F^.Blocks[I].Preds, 0);
    SetLength(F^.Blocks[I].DomChildren, 0);
    SetLength(F^.Blocks[I].DF, 0);
  end;
  SetLength(F^.Blocks, 0);
  SetLength(F^.ImplicitDefs, 0);
  Dispose(F);
end;

end.

```

`LuaSSAPasses.pas`:

```pas
unit LuaSSAPasses;

{
  LuaSSAPasses.pas - SSA pass manager and built-in optimisation passes.

  Provides a lightweight pass manager that runs a sequence of named SSA
  transformation passes over a PSSAFunction.  Five built-in passes are
  included:

    1. TrivialPhiElim     - collapse trivial PHI nodes
    2. CopyPropagation    - forward MOVE sources, eliminate dead MOVEs
    3. ConstantFolding    - evaluate BINOP / UNOP on compile-time constants
    4. StrengthReduction  - simplify algebraic identities (x*1, x+0, ...)
    5. DeadCodeElim       - remove nodes whose results are never used

  All pass procedures are standalone (not class methods) so they can be
  registered individually or composed into custom pipelines.

  Usage:
    var PM: TSSAPassManager;
    PM := CreateDefaultPassManager;
    try
      PM.RunAll(SF);
    finally
      PM.Free;
    end;
}

{$mode objfpc}{$H+}

interface

uses
  SysUtils, LuaChunk, LuaSSA;

{ ======================================================================
  Global change flag -- passes set this to True when they modify the IR.
  Used by the pass manager for iterative convergence.
  ====================================================================== }
var
  GPassChanged: Boolean;

{ ======================================================================
  Pass manager
  ====================================================================== }
type
  TSSAPassProc = procedure(SF: PSSAFunction);

  TSSAPassInfo = record
    Name     : AnsiString;
    Proc     : TSSAPassProc;
    Iterative: Boolean;       { if True, run until no changes (max 10) }
  end;

  TSSAPassManager = class
  private
    FPasses: array of TSSAPassInfo;
    FCount : Integer;
    FDebug : Boolean;
  public
    constructor Create(Debug: Boolean = False);
    procedure AddPass(const Name: AnsiString; Proc: TSSAPassProc;
                      Iterative: Boolean = False);
    procedure RunAll(SF: PSSAFunction);
  end;

{ ======================================================================
  Built-in pass procedures
  ====================================================================== }

{ Replace SSA_MOVE defs with their source, eliminating redundant copies. }
procedure PassCopyPropagation(SF: PSSAFunction);

{ Collapse PHI nodes whose non-self-referencing operands are all identical. }
procedure PassTrivialPhiElim(SF: PSSAFunction);

{ Evaluate BINOP / UNOP on constant operands at compile time. }
procedure PassConstantFolding(SF: PSSAFunction);

{ Remove nodes whose Dest has zero uses (excluding side-effecting ops). }
procedure PassDeadCodeElim(SF: PSSAFunction);

{ Simplify algebraic identities like x*1, x+0, not not x. }
procedure PassStrengthReduction(SF: PSSAFunction);

{ ======================================================================
  Factory
  ====================================================================== }

{ Create a pass manager with the standard optimisation pipeline. }
function CreateDefaultPassManager(Debug: Boolean = False): TSSAPassManager;

implementation

{ ======================================================================
  TSSAPassManager
  ====================================================================== }

constructor TSSAPassManager.Create(Debug: Boolean);
begin
  inherited Create;
  FDebug := Debug;
  FCount := 0;
  SetLength(FPasses, 0);
end;

procedure TSSAPassManager.AddPass(const Name: AnsiString; Proc: TSSAPassProc;
                                  Iterative: Boolean);
begin
  Inc(FCount);
  SetLength(FPasses, FCount);
  FPasses[FCount - 1].Name      := Name;
  FPasses[FCount - 1].Proc      := Proc;
  FPasses[FCount - 1].Iterative := Iterative;
end;

procedure TSSAPassManager.RunAll(SF: PSSAFunction);
const
  MaxIter = 10;
var
  I, Iter: Integer;
begin
  for I := 0 to FCount - 1 do begin
    if FPasses[I].Iterative then begin
      { Run the pass repeatedly until it makes no changes, or MaxIter. }
      Iter := 0;
      repeat
        GPassChanged := False;
        if FDebug then
          WriteLn('[PassManager] Running "', FPasses[I].Name,
                  '" (iteration ', Iter + 1, ')');
        FPasses[I].Proc(SF);
        Inc(Iter);
      until (not GPassChanged) or (Iter >= MaxIter);
      if FDebug and (Iter >= MaxIter) then
        WriteLn('[PassManager] "', FPasses[I].Name,
                '" hit max iterations (', MaxIter, ')');
    end else begin
      GPassChanged := False;
      if FDebug then
        WriteLn('[PassManager] Running "', FPasses[I].Name, '"');
      FPasses[I].Proc(SF);
    end;
  end;
end;

{ ======================================================================
  Internal helpers
  ====================================================================== }

{ Return True when two refs address the same SSA value. }
function SameValue(const A, B: TSSARef): Boolean; inline;
begin
  Result := RefEqual(A, B);
end;

{ Return True if the node kind has side effects and must not be eliminated. }
function HasSideEffects(Kind: TSSAKind): Boolean;
begin
  Result := Kind in [SSA_CALL, SSA_TAILCALL, SSA_RETURN,
                     SSA_SETGLOBAL, SSA_SETTABLE, SSA_SETUPVAL,
                     SSA_SETLIST, SSA_CLOSE];
end;

{ Helper math: floor function matching Lua semantics. }
function LuaFloor(X: Double): Double;
begin
  Result := Int(X);
  if (X < 0) and (Frac(X) <> 0) then
    Result := Result - 1.0;
end;

{ Helper math: power function for constant folding. }
function LuaPower(Base, Exp: Double): Double;
begin
  if Exp = 0.0 then
    Result := 1.0
  else if (Base = 0.0) and (Exp > 0) then
    Result := 0.0
  else if (Frac(Exp) = 0) and (Abs(Exp) < 100) then begin
    { Integer exponent -- iterate to avoid precision issues. }
    Result := 1.0;
    if Exp > 0 then begin
      while Exp >= 1.0 do begin
        Result := Result * Base;
        Exp := Exp - 1.0;
      end;
    end else begin
      while Exp <= -1.0 do begin
        Result := Result / Base;
        Exp := Exp + 1.0;
      end;
    end;
  end else
    { General case -- use FPC built-in. }
    Result := System.Exp(Exp * System.Ln(Abs(Base)));
end;

{ ------------------------------------------------------------------
  Use-count map keyed by (Reg, Version).
  Implemented as a flat array of (Reg, Version, Count) triples with
  linear probing -- good enough for the small node counts we see.
  ------------------------------------------------------------------ }
type
  TUseEntry = record
    Reg, Version: Integer;
    Count       : Integer;
  end;

  TUseMap = record
    Entries: array of TUseEntry;
    Cap    : Integer;
    Len    : Integer;
  end;

procedure InitUseMap(out M: TUseMap; Cap: Integer);
var
  I: Integer;
begin
  M.Cap := Cap;
  M.Len := 0;
  SetLength(M.Entries, Cap);
  for I := 0 to Cap - 1 do begin
    M.Entries[I].Reg     := -1;
    M.Entries[I].Version := 0;
    M.Entries[I].Count   := 0;
  end;
end;

function UseMapHash(Reg, Ver, Cap: Integer): Integer;
var
  H: LongWord;
begin
  H := LongWord(Reg) * 2654435761 + LongWord(Ver) * 40503;
  Result := Integer(H mod LongWord(Cap));
end;

procedure IncUse(var M: TUseMap; Reg, Ver: Integer);
var
  H: Integer;
begin
  if Reg < 0 then Exit;  { not a register ref }
  H := UseMapHash(Reg, Ver, M.Cap);
  while True do begin
    if M.Entries[H].Reg = -1 then begin
      { empty slot -- insert }
      M.Entries[H].Reg     := Reg;
      M.Entries[H].Version := Ver;
      M.Entries[H].Count   := 1;
      Inc(M.Len);
      Exit;
    end;
    if (M.Entries[H].Reg = Reg) and (M.Entries[H].Version = Ver) then begin
      Inc(M.Entries[H].Count);
      Exit;
    end;
    H := (H + 1) mod M.Cap;
  end;
end;

function GetUseCount(const M: TUseMap; Reg, Ver: Integer): Integer;
var
  H: Integer;
begin
  Result := 0;
  if Reg < 0 then Exit;
  H := UseMapHash(Reg, Ver, M.Cap);
  while True do begin
    if M.Entries[H].Reg = -1 then Exit;
    if (M.Entries[H].Reg = Reg) and (M.Entries[H].Version = Ver) then begin
      Result := M.Entries[H].Count;
      Exit;
    end;
    H := (H + 1) mod M.Cap;
  end;
end;

{ Count a single ref into the use map. }
procedure CountRef(var M: TUseMap; const R: TSSARef); inline;
begin
  if R.Reg >= 0 then
    IncUse(M, R.Reg, R.Version);
end;

{ Rewrite every use of OldRef to NewRef across the entire function. }
procedure RewriteUses(SF: PSSAFunction; const OldRef, NewRef: TSSARef);

  procedure Patch(var R: TSSARef); inline;
  begin
    if SameValue(R, OldRef) then
      R := NewRef;
  end;

var
  I, J: Integer;
  N: PSSANode;
begin
  for I := 0 to High(SF^.AllNodes) do begin
    N := SF^.AllNodes[I];
    if N^.Kind = SSA_NOP then Continue;

    Patch(N^.OpA);
    Patch(N^.OpB);
    Patch(N^.OpC);

    for J := 0 to High(N^.ArgRefs) do
      Patch(N^.ArgRefs[J]);

    for J := 0 to High(N^.Operands) do
      Patch(N^.Operands[J]);
  end;
end;

{ Check whether any PHI node in the function uses the given ref. }
function UsedByPhi(SF: PSSAFunction; const R: TSSARef): Boolean;
var
  I, J: Integer;
  N: PSSANode;
begin
  Result := False;
  for I := 0 to High(SF^.AllNodes) do begin
    N := SF^.AllNodes[I];
    if N^.Kind <> SSA_PHI then Continue;
    for J := 0 to High(N^.Operands) do begin
      if SameValue(N^.Operands[J], R) then begin
        Result := True;
        Exit;
      end;
    end;
  end;
end;

{ Try to resolve a constant value from a proto's K table.
  Returns True if the ref is a constant and the value was extracted.
  OutKind indicates what type of constant it is. }
function ResolveConst(Proto: PProto; const R: TSSARef;
                      out OutKind: TLuaConstKind;
                      out NVal: Double; out IVal: Int64;
                      out SVal: AnsiString; out BVal: Boolean): Boolean;
var
  Idx: Integer;
begin
  Result := False;
  if R.Reg <> SSA_CONST_REG then Exit;
  Idx := R.ConstIdx;
  if (Idx < 0) or (Idx >= Length(Proto^.K)) then Exit;

  OutKind := Proto^.K[Idx].Kind;
  NVal    := Proto^.K[Idx].NValue;
  IVal    := Proto^.K[Idx].IValue;
  SVal    := Proto^.K[Idx].SValue;
  BVal    := Proto^.K[Idx].BValue;
  Result  := True;
end;

{ Convenience: resolve a numeric constant (integer or float/number).
  Returns True and sets Val if the ref is a numeric constant. }
function ResolveNumericConst(Proto: PProto; const R: TSSARef;
                             out Val: Double): Boolean;
var
  Kind: TLuaConstKind;
  NV: Double;
  IV: Int64;
  SV: AnsiString;
  BV: Boolean;
begin
  Result := False;
  if not ResolveConst(Proto, R, Kind, NV, IV, SV, BV) then Exit;
  case Kind of
    lckNumber, lckFloat: begin Val := NV; Result := True; end;
    lckInteger:          begin Val := IV; Result := True; end;
  end;
end;

{ ======================================================================
  Pass 1 -- Trivial PHI elimination
  ======================================================================
  A PHI node is trivial when all of its non-self-referencing operands
  resolve to the same SSA value.  In that case the PHI can be replaced
  by a simple MOVE (or NOP if the source equals the destination). }

procedure PassTrivialPhiElim(SF: PSSAFunction);
var
  I, J: Integer;
  N: PSSANode;
  Common: TSSARef;
  AllSame: Boolean;
begin
  for I := 0 to High(SF^.AllNodes) do begin
    N := SF^.AllNodes[I];
    if N^.Kind <> SSA_PHI then Continue;
    if Length(N^.Operands) = 0 then Continue;

    { Find the first non-self operand as the candidate common value. }
    Common := SSA_NO_REF;
    AllSame := True;

    for J := 0 to High(N^.Operands) do begin
      { Skip self-references (operand equals the PHI's own Dest). }
      if SameValue(N^.Operands[J], N^.Dest) then Continue;

      if Common.Reg = -1 then begin
        { First real operand -- set as the candidate. }
        Common := N^.Operands[J];
      end else if not SameValue(N^.Operands[J], Common) then begin
        AllSame := False;
        Break;
      end;
    end;

    { If all operands were self-refs, the PHI is unreachable -- nop it. }
    if Common.Reg = -1 then begin
      N^.Kind := SSA_NOP;
      GPassChanged := True;
      Continue;
    end;

    if not AllSame then Continue;

    { All non-self operands are identical.  Replace the PHI. }
    RewriteUses(SF, N^.Dest, Common);

    if SameValue(N^.Dest, Common) then
      N^.Kind := SSA_NOP
    else begin
      N^.Kind := SSA_MOVE;
      N^.OpA  := Common;
      SetLength(N^.Operands, 0);
      SetLength(N^.PhiBlocks, 0);
    end;
    GPassChanged := True;
  end;
end;

{ ======================================================================
  Pass 2 -- Copy propagation
  ======================================================================
  For every MOVE node (Dest := OpA), replace all uses of Dest with OpA
  throughout the function, then convert the MOVE to NOP.  Skip if the
  source is not a valid register ref or if the Dest is used by a PHI
  node (PHI nodes are sensitive to register identity for control-flow
  recovery in the decompiler). }

procedure PassCopyPropagation(SF: PSSAFunction);
var
  I: Integer;
  N: PSSANode;
begin
  for I := 0 to High(SF^.AllNodes) do begin
    N := SF^.AllNodes[I];
    if N^.Kind <> SSA_MOVE then Continue;

    { Source must be a valid register reference. }
    if N^.OpA.Reg < 0 then Continue;

    { Do not propagate copies that are consumed by PHI nodes -- the
      decompiler's control-flow recovery depends on the register
      identity of PHI operands. }
    if UsedByPhi(SF, N^.Dest) then Continue;

    { Rewrite all uses of Dest -> OpA and kill the MOVE. }
    RewriteUses(SF, N^.Dest, N^.OpA);
    N^.Kind := SSA_NOP;
    GPassChanged := True;
  end;
end;

{ ======================================================================
  Pass 3 -- Constant folding
  ======================================================================
  Evaluate BINOP and UNOP nodes whose operands are compile-time
  constants.  The result is stored as a new synthetic constant in the
  proto's K table and the node is rewritten to SSA_LOADK. }

procedure PassConstantFolding(SF: PSSAFunction);
var
  I: Integer;
  N: PSSANode;
  ValA, ValB, ValR: Double;
  Proto: PProto;
  NewIdx: Integer;
  Kind: TLuaConstKind;
  NV: Double;
  IV: Int64;
  SV: AnsiString;
  BV: Boolean;
  BoolResult: Boolean;
begin
  Proto := SF^.Proto;

  for I := 0 to High(SF^.AllNodes) do begin
    N := SF^.AllNodes[I];

    { --- BINOP constant folding --- }
    if N^.Kind = SSA_BINOP then begin
      if not ResolveNumericConst(Proto, N^.OpA, ValA) then Continue;
      if not ResolveNumericConst(Proto, N^.OpB, ValB) then Continue;

      case N^.BinOp of
        bopAdd:  ValR := ValA + ValB;
        bopSub:  ValR := ValA - ValB;
        bopMul:  ValR := ValA * ValB;
        bopDiv:  begin
          if ValB = 0 then Continue;  { avoid division by zero }
          ValR := ValA / ValB;
        end;
        bopMod:  begin
          if ValB = 0 then Continue;
          { Lua mod: a - floor(a/b)*b }
          ValR := ValA - LuaFloor(ValA / ValB) * ValB;
        end;
        bopPow:  ValR := LuaPower(ValA, ValB);
        bopIDiv: begin
          if ValB = 0 then Continue;
          ValR := LuaFloor(ValA / ValB);
        end;
      else
        Continue;  { unsupported binop for folding }
      end;

      { Choose integer or float constant based on result. }
      if (Frac(ValR) = 0) and (ValR >= Low(Int64)) and
         (ValR <= High(Int64)) then
        NewIdx := AddSyntheticConst(Proto, Trunc(ValR))
      else
        NewIdx := AddSyntheticFloatConst(Proto, ValR);

      { Rewrite node to LOADK. }
      N^.Kind     := SSA_LOADK;
      N^.ConstIdx := NewIdx;
      N^.OpA      := SSA_NO_REF;
      N^.OpB      := SSA_NO_REF;
      GPassChanged := True;
      Continue;
    end;

    { --- UNOP constant folding --- }
    if N^.Kind = SSA_UNOP then begin
      if not ResolveConst(Proto, N^.OpA, Kind, NV, IV, SV, BV) then
        Continue;

      case N^.UnOp of
        uopNeg: begin
          { Negate a numeric constant. }
          if not (Kind in [lckNumber, lckFloat, lckInteger]) then Continue;
          if Kind = lckInteger then
            NewIdx := AddSyntheticConst(Proto, -IV)
          else
            NewIdx := AddSyntheticFloatConst(Proto, -NV);
        end;

        uopNot: begin
          { Boolean inversion.  In Lua, nil and false are falsy,
            everything else is truthy.  For constants we handle
            booleans and nil. }
          case Kind of
            lckNil:     BoolResult := True;
            lckBoolean: BoolResult := not BV;
          else
            { "not <number>" or "not <string>" is always false. }
            BoolResult := False;
          end;
          { Store as integer 0/1 constant (Lua LOADBOOL semantics). }
          NewIdx := AddSyntheticConst(Proto, Ord(BoolResult));
        end;

        uopLen: begin
          { String length. }
          if Kind <> lckString then Continue;
          NewIdx := AddSyntheticConst(Proto, Length(SV));
        end;

      else
        Continue;
      end;

      { Rewrite node to LOADK. }
      N^.Kind     := SSA_LOADK;
      N^.ConstIdx := NewIdx;
      N^.OpA      := SSA_NO_REF;
      GPassChanged := True;
    end;
  end;
end;

{ ======================================================================
  Pass 4 -- Strength reduction
  ======================================================================
  Simplify algebraic identities where one operand is a known constant:

    x * 1  =>  MOVE x         x * 0  =>  LOADK 0
    x + 0  =>  MOVE x         x - 0  =>  MOVE x
    not not x  =>  MOVE x     (double boolean negation)

  The pass also handles the commutative variants (1 * x, 0 + x). }

procedure PassStrengthReduction(SF: PSSAFunction);
var
  I, J: Integer;
  N: PSSANode;
  Proto: PProto;
  ValA, ValB: Double;
  AConst, BConst: Boolean;
  ZeroIdx: Integer;
  Inner: PSSANode;
begin
  Proto := SF^.Proto;

  for I := 0 to High(SF^.AllNodes) do begin
    N := SF^.AllNodes[I];

    { --- BINOP identities --- }
    if N^.Kind = SSA_BINOP then begin
      AConst := ResolveNumericConst(Proto, N^.OpA, ValA);
      BConst := ResolveNumericConst(Proto, N^.OpB, ValB);

      { At least one operand must be constant. }
      if not (AConst or BConst) then Continue;

      case N^.BinOp of
        bopMul: begin
          { x * 1 => MOVE x }
          if BConst and (ValB = 1.0) then begin
            N^.Kind := SSA_MOVE;
            { OpA already holds the non-constant operand. }
            N^.OpB  := SSA_NO_REF;
            GPassChanged := True;
          end
          { 1 * x => MOVE x }
          else if AConst and (ValA = 1.0) then begin
            N^.Kind := SSA_MOVE;
            N^.OpA  := N^.OpB;
            N^.OpB  := SSA_NO_REF;
            GPassChanged := True;
          end
          { x * 0 => LOADK 0 }
          else if BConst and (ValB = 0.0) then begin
            ZeroIdx := AddSyntheticConst(Proto, 0);
            N^.Kind     := SSA_LOADK;
            N^.ConstIdx := ZeroIdx;
            N^.OpA      := SSA_NO_REF;
            N^.OpB      := SSA_NO_REF;
            GPassChanged := True;
          end
          { 0 * x => LOADK 0 }
          else if AConst and (ValA = 0.0) then begin
            ZeroIdx := AddSyntheticConst(Proto, 0);
            N^.Kind     := SSA_LOADK;
            N^.ConstIdx := ZeroIdx;
            N^.OpA      := SSA_NO_REF;
            N^.OpB      := SSA_NO_REF;
            GPassChanged := True;
          end;
        end;

        bopAdd: begin
          { x + 0 => MOVE x }
          if BConst and (ValB = 0.0) then begin
            N^.Kind := SSA_MOVE;
            N^.OpB  := SSA_NO_REF;
            GPassChanged := True;
          end
          { 0 + x => MOVE x }
          else if AConst and (ValA = 0.0) then begin
            N^.Kind := SSA_MOVE;
            N^.OpA  := N^.OpB;
            N^.OpB  := SSA_NO_REF;
            GPassChanged := True;
          end;
        end;

        bopSub: begin
          { x - 0 => MOVE x }
          if BConst and (ValB = 0.0) then begin
            N^.Kind := SSA_MOVE;
            N^.OpB  := SSA_NO_REF;
            GPassChanged := True;
          end;
          { Note: 0 - x is negation, NOT identity -- do not reduce. }
        end;

      end; { case BinOp }
      Continue;
    end;

    { --- UNOP: double boolean negation (not not x) --- }
    if (N^.Kind = SSA_UNOP) and (N^.UnOp = uopNot) then begin
      { Check if the operand is itself a "not" node. }
      if N^.OpA.Reg < 0 then Continue;

      { Find the defining node of OpA by scanning AllNodes. }
      Inner := nil;
      for J := 0 to High(SF^.AllNodes) do begin
        if SF^.AllNodes[J]^.Kind = SSA_NOP then Continue;
        if SameValue(SF^.AllNodes[J]^.Dest, N^.OpA) then begin
          Inner := SF^.AllNodes[J];
          Break;
        end;
      end;

      if (Inner <> nil) and (Inner^.Kind = SSA_UNOP) and
         (Inner^.UnOp = uopNot) then begin
        { not (not x) => MOVE x }
        N^.Kind := SSA_MOVE;
        N^.OpA  := Inner^.OpA;
        GPassChanged := True;
      end;
    end;
  end;
end;

{ ======================================================================
  Pass 5 -- Dead code elimination
  ======================================================================
  Count uses for every SSA value.  Any node whose Dest has zero uses
  and no side effects is converted to SSA_NOP.  PHI self-references
  are excluded from the use count (a PHI that only references itself
  is considered dead). }

procedure PassDeadCodeElim(SF: PSSAFunction);
var
  UseMap: TUseMap;
  I, J, Cap: Integer;
  N: PSSANode;
  UseCnt: Integer;
begin
  { Build use-count map, counting all nodes. }
  Cap := Length(SF^.AllNodes) * 4 + 16;
  InitUseMap(UseMap, Cap);

  for I := 0 to High(SF^.AllNodes) do begin
    N := SF^.AllNodes[I];
    if N^.Kind = SSA_NOP then Continue;

    CountRef(UseMap, N^.OpA);
    CountRef(UseMap, N^.OpB);
    CountRef(UseMap, N^.OpC);

    for J := 0 to High(N^.ArgRefs) do
      CountRef(UseMap, N^.ArgRefs[J]);

    { For PHI operands, count them -- self-refs will be subtracted below. }
    for J := 0 to High(N^.Operands) do
      CountRef(UseMap, N^.Operands[J]);
  end;

  { Now eliminate dead nodes. }
  for I := 0 to High(SF^.AllNodes) do begin
    N := SF^.AllNodes[I];
    if N^.Kind = SSA_NOP then Continue;
    if N^.Dest.Reg < 0 then Continue;    { no destination -- skip }

    { Never eliminate side-effecting nodes. }
    if HasSideEffects(N^.Kind) then Continue;

    { Also preserve control-flow nodes. }
    if N^.Kind in [SSA_JUMP, SSA_BRANCH, SSA_FORPREP, SSA_FORLOOP,
                   SSA_TFORLOOP] then Continue;

    { Get the raw use count. }
    UseCnt := GetUseCount(UseMap, N^.Dest.Reg, N^.Dest.Version);

    { Subtract self-references for PHI nodes. }
    if N^.Kind = SSA_PHI then begin
      for J := 0 to High(N^.Operands) do begin
        if SameValue(N^.Operands[J], N^.Dest) then
          Dec(UseCnt);
      end;
    end;

    { If no external uses, the node is dead. }
    if UseCnt <= 0 then begin
      N^.Kind := SSA_NOP;
      GPassChanged := True;
    end;
  end;
end;

{ ======================================================================
  Factory function
  ====================================================================== }

function CreateDefaultPassManager(Debug: Boolean): TSSAPassManager;
begin
  Result := TSSAPassManager.Create(Debug);

  { Standard pipeline order:
    1. Eliminate trivial PHIs first -- simplifies the graph.
    2. Propagate copies -- reduces MOVEs.
    3. Fold constants -- evaluate compile-time expressions.
    4. Reduce strength -- simplify algebraic patterns.
    5. Eliminate dead code -- clean up unused nodes last. }
  Result.AddPass('TrivialPhiElim',    @PassTrivialPhiElim);
  Result.AddPass('CopyPropagation',   @PassCopyPropagation);
  Result.AddPass('ConstantFolding',   @PassConstantFolding);
  Result.AddPass('StrengthReduction', @PassStrengthReduction);
  Result.AddPass('DeadCodeElim',      @PassDeadCodeElim);
end;

end.

```

`LuaUtils.pas`:

```pas
Unit LuaUtils;

{$mode ObjFPC}{$H+}

Interface

Uses
  SysUtils;

Function EscapeString(Const S: AnsiString): AnsiString;

Implementation

Function EscapeString(Const S: AnsiString): AnsiString;
Var
  i: Integer;

  Function ByteEscape(B: Byte): AnsiString;
  Begin
    Result := '\' +
              Chr(Ord('0') + (B div 100)) +
              Chr(Ord('0') + ((B div 10) mod 10)) +
              Chr(Ord('0') + (B mod 10));
  End;

Begin
  Result := '';
  For i := 1 To Length(S) Do
    Case S[i] Of
      #10: Result += '\n';
      #13: Result += '\r';
      #9: Result += '\t';
      '\': Result += '\\';
      '"': Result += '\"';
      Else
        If (Ord(S[i]) < 32) Or (Ord(S[i]) > 126) Then
          Result += ByteEscape(Ord(S[i]))
        Else
          Result += S[i];
    End;
End;

End.

```

`README.md`:

```md
# cLuaDecompiler

[![License: AGPL v3](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-AGPL%20v3-blue.svg)](https://www.gnu.org/licenses/agpl-3.0)

cLuaDecompiler is a Free Pascal/Lazarus Lua bytecode disassembler and decompiler
for Lua 5.1, 5.2, 5.3, 5.4, and 5.5 binary chunks.

The project focuses on version-aware bytecode parsing, semantic opcode mapping,
SSA-based analysis, and Lua source emission that can be validated against the
original program behavior when source is available.

## Features

- Disassembles Lua bytecode with version-aware opcode names and operands.
- Decompiles `.luac` files to Lua-like source.
- Dumps SSA IR for debugging control flow, register lifetimes, phi nodes, and
  decompiler decisions.
- Supports built-in opcode tables for Lua 5.1 through 5.5.
- Supports custom opcode-table loading for patched or obfuscated bytecode.
- Includes runtime validation that compares original source output with
  decompiled output under the matching Lua version.
- Includes source-less bytecode validation for cases where opcode/disassembly
  coverage is the source of truth.
- Includes an optional VS Code extension for opening `.luac` files as
  decompiled Lua text.

## Decompilation Examples

- Check the `examples` folder.

## Download

- Download it from [Releases](https://github.com/Coldzer0/LuaDecompiler/releases)

## Requirements

- Windows or another platform supported by Free Pascal/Lazarus.
- Lazarus with `LazBuild` available in `PATH`.
- Python 3 for the validation scripts.
- Lua and `luac` binaries for validation, placed under the expected local
  layout when running the full suite:

```text
lua/lua5.1/lua5.1.exe
lua/lua5.1/luac5.1.exe
lua/lua5.2/lua52.exe
lua/lua5.2/luac52.exe
lua/lua5.3/lua53.exe
lua/lua5.3/luac53.exe
lua/lua5.4/lua54.exe
lua/lua5.4/luac54.exe
lua/lua5.5/lua55.exe
lua/lua5.5/luac55.exe
```

## Build

Build from the repository root with Lazarus:

```powershell
LazBuild cLuaDecompiler.lpi
```

The main executable is produced as:

```text
cLuaDecompiler.exe
```

## Usage

```powershell
.\cLuaDecompiler.exe [options] <file.luac>
```

Common commands:

```powershell
.\cLuaDecompiler.exe --dis sample.luac
.\cLuaDecompiler.exe --dec sample.luac
.\cLuaDecompiler.exe --ssa-dump sample.luac
.\cLuaDecompiler.exe --dec --annotate sample.luac
```

Options:

```text
--dis              Disassemble bytecode
--dec              Decompile to Lua-like source; default mode
--ssa-dump         Dump SSA IR for all protos
--annotate         Add disassembly comments during decompilation
--debug            Emit debug trace to stderr
--lua-version VER  Override detected Lua version: 51, 52, 53, 54, or 55
--opcode-table F   Load a custom opcode mapping from file F
--help             Show CLI help
```

Examples:

```powershell
.\cLuaDecompiler.exe --dec script.luac > script.decompiled.lua
.\cLuaDecompiler.exe --dis script.luac > script.dis.txt
.\cLuaDecompiler.exe --ssa-dump script.luac > script.ssa.txt
.\cLuaDecompiler.exe --lua-version 51 --opcode-table opcode_tables\custom.txt patched.luac
```

## Testing

Run the runtime and bytecode validation suite:

```powershell
python run_decompiler_validation.py
```

Compile local Lua 5.1 fixtures manually:

```powershell
tests\compile_luac.bat
```

`run_decompiler_validation.py` is the preferred validation path for decompiler
correctness because it can:

- compile source fixtures with the matching Lua version,
- run the original source,
- decompile the compiled bytecode,
- run the decompiled output,
- compare runtime output,
- compile stripped-bytecode fixtures,
- validate source-less bytecode through disassembly and opcode signatures.

Useful validation filters:

```powershell
python run_decompiler_validation.py --versions 51
python run_decompiler_validation.py --timeout 10 --out-dir decompiled\validation_tmp
```

## Adding Test Cases

Runtime-equivalence fixtures should either live in:

```text
validation/fixtures/runtime/
```

or be referenced from:

```text
validation/runtime_cases.json
```

Fixtures can declare supported versions at the top of the Lua file:

```lua
-- versions: 51 52 53 54 55
```

Source-less bytecode cases belong in:

```text
validation/bytecode_cases.json
```

Use `validation/stubs/` for small fake environments needed to load or compile
decompiled source outside the original host application. For bytecode without
source, compare disassembly and opcode signatures rather than pretending there
is a source-level oracle.

Do not commit generated `.luac` files from `compiled/` or decompiler output from
`decompiled/`.

## Architecture Notes

The main pipeline is:

```text
LuaChunk.pas -> LuaOpcodes.pas -> LuaSSA.pas -> LuaDecomp.pas/LuaDecomp*.inc
```

When adding support for a Lua version or opcode pattern:

1. Verify chunk and instruction decoding in `LuaChunk.pas`.
2. Add or correct semantic opcode mapping in `LuaOpcodes.pas`.
3. Lift the semantic operation in `LuaSSA.pas`.
4. Prefer fixing value/control-flow facts in SSA before patching emitted text.
5. Update decompiler emission in the matching `LuaDecomp*.inc` file.
6. Add runtime validation or source-less bytecode validation.

Useful debug flow:

```powershell
.\cLuaDecompiler.exe --dis sample.luac > sample.dis.txt
.\cLuaDecompiler.exe --ssa-dump sample.luac > sample.ssa.txt
.\cLuaDecompiler.exe --dec --annotate sample.luac > sample.lua
```

The official Lua source browser is useful when opcode behavior or chunk format
details are unclear:

```text
https://www.lua.org/source/
```

## VS Code Extension

The optional extension in `vscode_plugin/` opens `.luac` files through
`cLuaDecompiler.exe` and presents the result as normal Lua text so the existing
Lua syntax highlighter can be used.

The extension can auto-detect `cLuaDecompiler.exe` near the workspace, or you can
set:

```text
cluaDecompiler.executablePath
```

## Current Limitations

Decompiler output is best treated as recovered source, not guaranteed original
source formatting. Local variable names, closure names, control-flow shape, and
expression ordering may differ from the original program while still preserving
runtime behavior.

Stripped bytecode and source-less application bytecode should be validated with
runtime stubs when possible and with disassembly/opcode coverage when no source
oracle exists.

## References

Some Of the analyses and algorithms in this project are based on the following public
sources:

- Keith D. Cooper, Timothy J. Harvey, and Ken Kennedy, *A Simple, Fast
  Dominance Algorithm*, Rice University, 2001 - iterative dominator and
  immediate-dominator computation.
- Ron Cytron, Jeanne Ferrante, Barry K. Rosen, Mark N. Wegman, and F. Kenneth
  Zadeck, *Efficiently Computing Static Single Assignment Form and the
  Control Dependence Graph*, ACM TOPLAS 13(4), 1991 - dominance frontiers,
  φ-function placement, and the SSA rename pass.
- Keith D. Cooper and Linda Torczon, *Engineering a Compiler* - background
  material on iterative dataflow analysis used as the basis for the
  dominator and SSA construction passes.
- The official Lua source browser at <https://www.lua.org/source/>, including
  the `lopcodes.h`, `lopcodes.c`, and `lvm.c` files for Lua 5.1, 5.2, 5.3,
  5.4, and 5.5 - authoritative opcode tables, instruction formats, and VM
  semantics.
- The `unluac` Lua decompiler project - used as an external comparison
  oracle for output formatting and structural recovery on shared fixtures.
